Title: Duplicate meta tags injected by SPA — SEO conflict
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Open https://www.insightvm.com
2. View page source (Ctrl+U)
3. Search for '<meta name="description">'
4. Count occurrences
Expected Result: Each meta tag (description, og:title, og:description, etc.) should appear exactly once
Actual Result: The page has DUPLICATE meta tags: the static HTML template includes og:title, og:description, og:url, og:locale, twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description AND the React SPA injects a second set. Search engines may use either set, causing inconsistent SEO results.
Suggested Fix: Remove duplicate meta tags from either the static index.html or the React helmet/head manager. Use only one source of truth for meta tags.

Title: Footer social links contain dead '#' href links
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Open https://www.insightvm.com
2. Scroll to footer
3. Inspect social media icon links
4. Check href attributes
Expected Result: All footer links should point to valid URLs
Actual Result: Two social link icons in the footer have href='#' (likely placeholder for LinkedIn and Twitter/X that were never configured). These are non-functional dead links.
Suggested Fix: Either add the correct LinkedIn/Twitter URLs or remove the placeholder icons entirely.

Title: Structured data claims LinkedIn but no LinkedIn link exists
Steps to Reproduce:
1. View page source
2. Find JSON-LD structured data script
3. Check 'sameAs' array
4. Compare with actual footer social links
Expected Result: Structured data 'sameAs' URLs should match actual social media profiles linked on the site
Actual Result: JSON-LD 'sameAs' includes 'https://linkedin.com/company/insightvisionmedia' but no LinkedIn link exists on the site. Only Facebook and Instagram are present.
Suggested Fix: Update JSON-LD structured data to only include social profiles that actually exist and are linked on the site.

Title: No hamburger menu on mobile viewport (375x667)
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Open https://www.insightvm.com on a 375x667 mobile viewport
2. Look for a hamburger/toggle menu button in the header
3. Check if navigation links are accessible
Expected Result: A hamburger menu icon should be visible on mobile to toggle navigation. Full desktop nav should not be shown.
Actual Result: No hamburger menu button detected by automation selectors (button[aria-label*='menu'], [class*='hamburger'], [class*='burger'], [class*='menu-toggle']). Navigation may overflow or be hidden on small screens.
Suggested Fix: Implement a responsive hamburger menu with proper aria-label='Toggle menu' for viewports below 768px.

Title: 27 clickable elements smaller than 44x44px on mobile
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Open site on mobile viewport (375x667)
2. Measure all interactive elements (buttons and links)
3. Check against WCAG 44x44px minimum tap target
Expected Result: All interactive elements should be at least 44x44px per WCAG 2.1 Level AA guidelines
Actual Result: 27 elements are below the 44x44px minimum. Includes: unnamed button (40x36px), 'Learn More' buttons (279x40px — height too short), and other interactive elements.
Screenshot/Video: test-results/artifacts/ — console warnings in test output
Suggested Fix: Increase min-height to 44px on all buttons and interactive elements. Add padding to reach minimum tap target size.

Title: No contact form on homepage — only on /contact page
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Open https://www.insightvm.com
2. Search for any <form> element on the page
3. Scroll through entire page
Expected Result: Homepage should have a contact form or clear inline form for lead generation
Actual Result: The homepage has NO form elements. Forms are only available on the /contact page. The CTA buttons ('Get Free Consultation', 'Book Strategy Call') link to /contact but don't provide inline form convenience.
Suggested Fix: Consider adding an inline contact form or email capture on the homepage to reduce friction for lead generation.

Title: Instagram profile URL inconsistency with structured data
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Check footer Instagram link
2. Check JSON-LD structured data sameAs array
3. Compare Instagram URLs
Expected Result: Instagram URLs should be consistent across the site
Actual Result: Footer links to 'https://www.instagram.com/insight.vm' but structured data 'sameAs' references 'https://instagram.com/insightvisionmedia'. These are different Instagram profiles/handles.
Suggested Fix: Unify Instagram URLs: use the same profile URL in both the footer link and the JSON-LD structured data.

Title: OG locale mismatch between static and dynamic meta tags
Steps to Reproduce:
1. View page source
2. Find og:locale meta tags
3. Compare values
Expected Result: A single og:locale should be defined matching the page language
Actual Result: Static HTML sets og:locale to 'tr_TR' (Turkish) but the React-injected meta sets og:locale to 'en_US' (English). The page content is in English. Having conflicting locale signals confuses search engines.
Suggested Fix: Set og:locale to 'en_US' consistently since the page content is in English. Remove the 'tr_TR' locale from static HTML.

Title: Verify: Contact form validation for empty required fields
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Navigate to https://www.insightvm.com/contact
2. Leave all fields empty
3. Click Submit/Send button
Expected Result: Form should show validation errors for required fields and prevent submission
Actual Result: Needs testing — run tests/contact-form.spec.ts to verify
Suggested Fix: Implement client-side validation for all required form fields.

Title: Verify: Page performance and large asset optimization
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Navigate to https://www.insightvm.com
2. Measure page load time and total transfer size
3. Check for oversized images
Expected Result: Page should load within 3s (DOMContentLoaded) and total size under 5MB
Actual Result: Needs testing — run tests/performance.spec.ts to verify
Suggested Fix: Optimize images (WebP/AVIF), enable lazy loading, minify CSS/JS.
